news.title: PepsiCo Bottling Shift In Northern Europe And What It Means For Valuation
news.summary: PepsiCo plans to end its bottling and distribution partnership with Royal Unibrew in Denmark, Finland, and the Baltic states in 2028. The change will affect how PepsiCo products are produced and distributed across key Northern European markets. The company will need to put new arrangements in place for local production, supply chains, and customer relationships once the agreement concludes. For in

news.title: PepsiCo Hands Northern Europe Bottling To Carlsberg What It Means For Investors
news.summary: PepsiCo (NasdaqGS:PEP) has chosen Carlsberg as its future bottling and distribution partner in Northern Europe. The agreement covers Denmark, Finland, and the Baltics and is set to take effect from 2029. news.title: Pepsi Picks Its Winner and Royal Unibrew Pays the Price
news.summary: Sometimes a contract loss is just a contract loss. And sometimes it’s a signal that your position in the value chain is weaker than you thought.

news.title: Carlsberg gains more PepsiCo rights as Royal Unibrew contract ends
news.summary: Royal Unibrew said it "would have welcomed the opportunity to continue the partnership; however, it has not been possible to reach an agreement".

news.title: Carlsberg Replaces Coke With Pepsi In Northern Europe, Royal Unibrew Shares Drop 20%
news.summary: Carlsberg will become PepsiCo's bottler in Denmark, Finland, and the Baltics from 2029, ending Coca-Cola agreements and expanding its footprint to 14 markets.
